texto: I'm not formed by things that are of myself alone. I wear my father's belt tied around my mother's blouse, and shoes which are from my uncle. This is me. Just as a flower does not choose its color, we are not responsible for what we have come to be. Only once you realize this do you become free, and to become adult is to become free.
explicacao: Citação de India Stoker sobre temas ligados a familia. “I'm not formed by things that are of myself alone. I wear my father's belt tied around my mother'…” articula familia, fate, reflexao em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
